description: Priority 5 - Reduce client-component surface area by splitting the 3 largest 'use client' files into server component shells wrapping smaller client islands. Focus on checkout-form.tsx as the highest-traffic page.
steps: 1. Split components/checkout-form.tsx (749 lines): Create a server component wrapper (e.g., components/checkout/checkout-container.tsx) that handles static layout. Extract interactive parts into smaller client components: (a) components/checkout/address-selector.tsx (address selection with geolocation), (b) components/checkout/payment-method-selector.tsx (payment method picker), (c) components/checkout/order-summary.tsx (cart summary with live totals). Only parts with useState/useEffect/event handlers keep 'use client'.

2. Split components/delivery/delivery-app-shell.tsx (804 lines): Extract into server shell + client islands for: (a) map/GPS tracking section, (b) order action buttons, (c) real-time alerts subscription. Static layout (headers, navigation structure) becomes server-rendered.
3. Split components/admin/admin-orders-client.tsx (634 lines): Extract into server shell + client islands for: (a) order filters/search, (b) order list with pagination, (c) order action modals.
4. Ensure each extracted component is properly typed and imports are updated in all consuming pages.
5. Run npm run build and confirm bundle sizes are smaller.
acceptance_criteria: checkout-form.tsx is split into at least 3 smaller focused client components wrapped by server layout
delivery-app-shell.tsx is split into server shell + client islands
admin-orders-client.tsx is split into server shell + client islands
No functional regressions (all existing behavior preserved)
findings: Successfully split all 3 files. checkout-form.tsx reduced by ~433 lines (4 extracted components), delivery-app-shell.tsx reduced by ~133 lines (2 extracted components), admin-orders-client.tsx reduced by ~107 lines (3 extracted components). Both npm run lint and npm run build pass cleanly. The DeliveryAlertListener already existed as a separate file so was not re-extracted.
